description_plain: Your child can connect what happens in a story or play to a picture or spoken version, and point out where each matches the original text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories are told, including the difference between stories told by a character (first person) and stories told by a narrator (third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems and drama when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and regular stories, and talk about the special parts of poems and plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in literature.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including those that refer to famous characters from literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail, using specific information from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and summarize what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a story to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts handle similar themes or topics to build understanding or see how authors approach them differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the arguments and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information shown in different ways, like pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how parts of a text, like sentences or paragraphs, fit together and support the whole piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or figurative meanings, and see how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main idea or theme of a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses about it, and use examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to help figure out or correct words they don't know, and will reread if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and for a clear reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about sounds, syllables, and word parts to read long, unfamiliar words both in and out of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use grade-level phonics and word skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or charts to their presentations to help explain their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear report or opinion, organize their ideas in order, use facts and details, and speak at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what a speaker says and explain how their points are supported by reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information they hear or see in different ways, like from a book read aloud, a video, or a chart.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and draw conclusions based on what was learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions in a discussion and add helpful comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for group discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or studied the topic, and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak for different situations and use formal English when it's needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visual aids to help explain information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and how they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships.
description_plain: Your child can correctly use grade-level words, including words that show contrast, addition, or other relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to help understand what each word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain the meaning of common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what similes and metaphors mean in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses, and other reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses (online or in print) to find out how to say a word and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast different types of English used in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can change sentences by expanding, combining, or shortening them to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references to check spelling if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show the titles of books and other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no, to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence, and to indicate direct address.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly when writing yes or no, asking a question at the end of a sentence, or speaking directly to someone in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after the first part of a sentence when it introduces what comes next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use pairs of words like either/or and neither/nor correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb tense changes incorrectly in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different verb tenses to show when things happen or their order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use perfect verb tenses like 'I have walked,' 'I had walked,' and 'I will have walked' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences and point out how they work in examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can use a wide range of general and subject-specific words and can figure out the meaning of new words when reading or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words relate to each other, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown words by using clues in the sentence, breaking down the word, or looking it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation and can make good choices about words and style when reading, writing, or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write often, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes writing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find important information from their own experiences or from books and websites, summarize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words and details that help the reader picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, description, and pacing to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting up the situation, introducing the narrator and characters, and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a logical sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas within and between sections of their writing using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by adding facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting, illustrations, and multimedia when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, give an overview, group related information, and use things like headings, pictures, or media to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic and share ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that matches the opinion they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic or text, state their opinion, and organize their ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces about topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in just one sitting, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to explain their ideas, reflect, or do research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if it's trustworthy, and use it in their own words without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way to say things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good details and organizing the events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using clear and accurate information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to support their opinions or claims, using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4-5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles about history, science, and other subjects at a fifth-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different books or articles on the same topic to write or talk knowledgeably about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points, and can identify which reasons support which points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different books or websites to quickly find answers or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can compare different accounts of the same event or topic and notice important similarities and differences in how they are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized, such as how events or ideas are arranged.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of general and subject-specific words and phrases in a fifth-grade text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, ideas, or concepts are connected in a nonfiction text, using specific information from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a text to explain what it says and to support their own ideas or conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4-5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a fifth-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast stories in the same genre to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, videos, or other media add to the meaning, mood, or beauty of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the narrator's or speaker's point of view affects how the story is told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to form the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including metaphors and similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in a story or play, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme of a story, play, or poem using details from the text, explain how characters respond to challenges, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a story or poem to explain what it says and to support their own ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts handle similar themes or topics to build understanding or see how authors approach them differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain and judge the arguments and claims in a text, including whether the reasons and evidence are strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is said and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, fit together and support the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or figurative language, and explain how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses, and use specific examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of reading strategies to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use different reading strategies to check and fix mistakes when reading, and will reread if needed to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with a clear reason and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they can understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about sounds, word parts, and word patterns to read long, unfamiliar words both in and out of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses phonics and word skills to figure out new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data sets by counting the number of objects in each category and interpret results by classifying and sorting objects by count.
description_plain: Your child can count how many objects are in each group and sort or classify them based on the number.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can look at groups of objects, show the information, and talk about what it means.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom. Collect or consider data that can naturally answer questions by sorting and counting.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about things in the classroom and collect or use data by sorting and counting to find answers.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to investigate and gather information by sorting or counting.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, and describe which object has "more" or "less" of the attribute.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects that share a feature, like length or weight, and say which one has more or less of that feature.

subject: Math
description: Describe several measurable attributes of a single object using measurable terms, such as length or weight.
description_plain: Your child can describe things about an object, like how long or heavy it is, using measurement words.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can talk about and compare things you can measure, like size or weight.

subject: Math
description: Compose common shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Represent shapes in the world by building shapes from components and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build and draw shapes they see in the world using smaller shapes.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts and attributes.
description_plain: Your child can look at different shapes and talk about how they are alike or different, using everyday words.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
description_plain: Your child can look at, compare, make, and build shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional or three-dimensional.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (2D) or solid (3D).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name common two-dimensional and three-dimensional geometric shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can correctly name common flat and solid shapes, no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes and describe the relative positions of these objects in their environment.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes they see around them and describe where things are using words like 'above,' 'below,' or 'next to.'

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe shapes.
description_plain: Your child can find and talk about shapes.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose from 11 to 19 into groups of ten ones and some further ones using objects, drawings, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers from 11 to 19 into a group of ten and some extra ones using objects, pictures, or simple math.

subject: Math
description: Work with numbers 11-19 to gain foundations for place value.
description_plain: Your child can work with numbers 11 to 19 to start learning about place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can look at two written numbers between 1 and 10 and tell which is bigger or if they are the same.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group.
description_plain: Your child can compare two groups of objects and say which group has more, less, or the same number.

subject: Math
description: Compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare numbers to see which is bigger, smaller, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions using up to 20 objects arranged in a variety of configurations or as 10 objects in a scattered configuration. Given a number from 1-20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects in different arrangements and can count out a certain number of objects when asked.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers tell how many things are in a group and can match counting to the right amount.

subject: Math
description: Count to tell the number of objects.
description_plain: Your child can count objects to find out how many there are.

subject: Math
description: Identify number names, write numbers, and the count sequence from 0-20. Represent a number of objects with a written number 0-20.
description_plain: Your child can say, write, and put numbers in order from 0 to 20, and can show a group of objects with the right number.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within 100 of a known sequence.
description_plain: Your child can start counting from any number up to 100, not just from 1.

subject: Math
description: Orally count to 100 by ones and by tens in sequential order.
description_plain: Your child can count out loud to 100 by ones and by tens in order.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the count sequence.
description_plain: Your child knows number names and can count in order.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5 with accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 5 in different ways.

subject: Math
description: By using objects, drawings, or equations, find the unknown number that makes 10 when added to a given number from 1 - 9.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what number needs to be added to another number (from 1 to 9) to make 10, using objects, pictures, or math.

subject: Math
description: Using objects or drawings, and equations, decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 10 into two groups in more than one way using objects, pictures, or math.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 10. Model authentic contexts and solve problems that use addition and subtraction within 10.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 10 and solve simple real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition as putting together and adding to and subtraction as taking apart and taking from using objects, drawings, physical expressions, numbers or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition as putting things together and subtraction as taking things apart, using objects, drawings, or numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can understand what it means to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns when solving problems and explain what they see happening over and over.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can spot how numbers and shapes are put together and use that to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child will use clear and careful math language and answers.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like blocks or rulers, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to show and solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to others' ideas to see if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers in different ways, using pictures or words to explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will keep trying to solve math problems, even if they are hard at first.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data sets with up to three categories by representing data visually, such as with graphs and charts, and interpret information presented to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can sort information into up to three groups, show it with simple graphs or charts, and answer questions about what they see.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can look at information, show it in pictures or charts, and explain what it means.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom. Collect or consider data that can naturally answer questions by representing data visually.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about things around them, collect information, and show it with pictures or charts.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to help find answers.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write the time to the hour and half-hour using both regular and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write the time.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of non-standard length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects end to end and counting them.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three things in order by length and compare two things by using a third object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can talk about and compare things like length or size.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares. Describe the equal shares and understand that partitioning into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can split circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts, describe the parts, and understand that more parts means smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Compose common two-dimensional shapes or three-dimensional shapes to create a composite shape, and create additional new shapes from composite shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make new shapes, and take apart shapes to make different ones.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between defining attributes versus non-defining attributes for a wide variety of shapes. Build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child can tell what makes a shape special, and can build or draw shapes with those features.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and what makes them different or the same.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 using concrete or visual representations and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can subtract groups of ten (like 20, 30, 40) from other groups of ten, using objects or pictures to help.

subject: Math
description: Without having to count, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than a given two-digit number and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly figure out what number is 10 more or 10 less than a given two-digit number and explain how they know.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100 using concrete or visual representations and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction. Relate the strategy to a written method and explain why sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100 using objects or pictures, explain how they did it, and understand when to make a new group of ten.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of tens and ones to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at the tens and ones, and use symbols like >, =, and < to show which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Understand 10 as a bundle of ten ones and that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 ones make a ten, and that two-digit numbers are made of tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers are made from tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
description_plain: Your child can count, read, and write numbers up to 120, starting from any number less than 120, and match numbers to groups of objects.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Your child can count forward from any number up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the missing number in an addition or subtraction problem with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use the meaning of the equal sign to determine whether equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.
description_plain: Your child can tell if an addition or subtraction equation is true or false by understanding the equal sign.

subject: Math
description: Work with addition and subtraction equations.
description_plain: Your child can work with addition and subtraction equations.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10 with accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 10, and solve problems with numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can use counting to help with adding and subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve subtraction problems by figuring out what number needs to be added to reach a total.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about adding and subtracting to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the rules for adding and subtracting, and knows how they are connected.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20 using objects, drawings or equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by adding three numbers together, as long as the total is 20 or less, using objects, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve and represent problems in authentic contexts involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction with numbers up to 20 to solve real-life problems, even when the missing number could be in any part of the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can show and solve problems using addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child will notice patterns in math and use them to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child will look for ways to break down math problems into smaller, easier parts.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child will use math words and numbers carefully and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child will choose the right tools, like rulers or counters, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child will use math to solve real-world problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to or question others' ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both pictures and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will keep trying different ways to solve a math problem until they find an answer.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data with a single-unit scale and interpret information presented to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at data shown with one unit (like tally marks or blocks) and use it to answer questions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can organize, show, and explain information using data.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom. Collect or consider data that can naturally answer questions by using measurements with whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions to explore in class and collect or use measurements to answer them.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to investigate and gather information to answer them.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in authentic contexts involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ (dollars) and c (cents) symbols appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems using coins and dollar bills, and write amounts using $ and ¢ symbols.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write the time to the nearest five minutes on both digital and analog clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.
description_plain: Your child can work with time and money in everyday situations.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole number lengths on a number line diagram; use number lines to find sums and differences within 100.
description_plain: Your child can show whole number lengths on a number line and use the line to add or subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve problems in authentic contexts involving lengths that are given in the same units.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction with numbers up to 100 to solve real-life problems about length.

subject: Math
description: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
description_plain: Your child can connect adding and subtracting to measuring length.

subject: Math
description: Measure two objects and determine the difference in their lengths in terms of a standard length unit.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can make good guesses about how long something is using inches, feet, yards, centimeters, or meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object using two different length units and describe how the measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure an object using two different units (like inches and centimeters) and explain how the size of the unit changes the measurement.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate measurement tools.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right tool and measure how long something is.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate lengths using standard units like inches or centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal parts. Recognize that equal parts of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can split circles and rectangles into 2, 3, or 4 equal parts and knows that equal parts can look different.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into equal rows and columns of squares and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw shapes with certain features, like a set number of corners or sides.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and their features.

subject: Math
description: Explain why strategies to add and subtract work using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can explain why their ways of adding and subtracting work, using what they know about how numbers work together.

subject: Math
description: Without having to count, mentally find 10 more or 10 less and 100 more or 100 less than a given three-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can quickly figure out 10 or 100 more or less than a three-digit number without counting each time.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1000 using concrete or visual representations and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction. Relate the strategy to a written method and explain why sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using objects, drawings, or place value strategies, and explain why sometimes they need to regroup tens or hundreds.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations and describe how two different strategies result in the same sum.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers using place value strategies and explain how different methods can give the same answer.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add & subtract within 100 using accurate, efficient, & flexible strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 100 using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of place value to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers by looking at the hundreds, tens, and ones, and use the symbols >, =, and < to show which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1000 in different ways, like with digits, words, or expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Count within 1000; skip-count by 5's, 10's, and 100's.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 1000 and skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s.

subject: Math
description: Understand 100 as a bundle of ten tens and that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that 100 is made of ten tens, and that each digit in a three-digit number shows hundreds, tens, or ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.
description_plain: Your child understands that each digit in a number has a different value depending on its place (ones, tens, hundreds).

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find out how many objects are in a rectangle with up to 5 rows and 5 columns, and can write an equation to show this.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group up to 20 objects has an odd or even number by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; record using drawings and equations including expressing an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects is odd or even by pairing them or counting by 2s, and can show this with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
description_plain: Your child works with equal groups of objects to start learning about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 20 using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step problems in authentic contexts by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life math problems using addition and subtraction with numbers up to 100, using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction to solve different kinds of problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child notices patterns in math and can explain how they repeat.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for ways that numbers and shapes are organized or connected.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate when solving math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child uses math to solve real-world problems and can show their thinking with pictures or numbers.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and discuss the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both pictures and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child keeps trying to solve math problems, even when they are hard.

subject: Math
description: Analyze measurement data with a scaled picture graph or a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Interpret information presented to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can use bar graphs or picture graphs with scales to show and understand measurement data, and answer questions about the information.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can look at data, show it in graphs, and explain what it means.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom, school or community. Collect or consider measurement data that can naturally answer questions by using information presented in a scaled picture and/or bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions about things around them, collect measurement data, and use graphs to help answer those questions.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather or think about data to find answers.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving authentic contexts for perimeters of polygons.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that involve finding the perimeter of shapes like rectangles and triangles.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: recognize perimeter.
description_plain: Your child understands what perimeter is and can find it for different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Relate area to multiplication and addition. Use relevant representations to solve problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication and addition to figure out the area of shapes and solve real-world problems about area.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting standard and non-standard unit squares.
description_plain: Your child can measure the area of a shape by counting squares, whether they are standard or not.

subject: Math
