description_plain: Your child understands what area is and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure, estimate and solve problems in authentic contexts that involve liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), and liters (l).
description_plain: Your child can measure, estimate, and solve problems about how much something weighs or how much liquid it holds using grams, kilograms, and liters.

subject: Math
description: Tell, write, and measure time to the nearest minute. Solve problems in authentic contexts that involve addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute and solve problems about adding or subtracting minutes.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring and estimating things like time, weight, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas and express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child can split shapes into equal parts and say what fraction of the whole each part is.

subject: Math
description: Understand that shapes in different categories may share attributes and that shared attributes can define a larger category.
description_plain: Your child understands that different shapes can have things in common, and these shared features can group them into bigger categories.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and their features to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases, and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can explain when two fractions are equal and compare fractions by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; Represent fractions on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child understands that fractions are numbers and can show them on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit fraction and explain how multiple copies of a unit fraction form a non-unit fraction.
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit fraction is and how combining them makes other fractions.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
description_plain: Your child learns that fractions are numbers and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Find the product of one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range 10-90, such as 9 x 80. Students use a range of strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a one-digit number by 10, 20, 30, and so on up to 90, using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 1,000 using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round whole numbers within 1000 to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round numbers up to 1,000 to the nearest 10 or 100.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child uses their understanding of place value and math rules to solve problems with bigger numbers.

subject: Math
description: Identify and explain arithmetic patterns using properties of operations, including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table.
description_plain: Your child can spot and explain patterns in addition and multiplication tables, like how numbers repeat or increase.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step problems in authentic contexts that use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division in equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life math problems that take two steps and use addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, even when a letter stands for a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations, and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, and can find and explain patterns in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100 using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately multiply and divide numbers up to 100 using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as an unknown-factor in a multiplication problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that division is like finding the missing number in a multiplication problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide.
description_plain: Your child uses rules about how numbers work to help solve multiplication and division problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand properties of multiplication and the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplication works and how it is connected to division.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers by applying the understanding of the inverse relationship of multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the missing number in a multiplication or division problem by knowing how the two operations are related.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division within 100 to solve problems in authentic contexts involving equal groups, arrays, and/or measurement quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication and division to solve real-world problems, like sharing things equally or arranging objects in groups or rows.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret whole-number quotients as dividing an amount into equal sized groups.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain how dividing means splitting a number into equal groups.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret multiplication of two factors as repeated addition of equal groups.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain that multiplying is like adding the same number over and over in equal groups.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child notices when things repeat in math and can explain the pattern.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for ways to organize and understand math problems by seeing how numbers and shapes are put together.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate when doing math.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child uses math to solve real-life problems and can show how math works in everyday situations.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and discuss other people's ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both pictures and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child keeps working on math problems, even when they are hard, and tries different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Analyze line plots to display a distribution of numerical measurement data, which include displays of data sets of fractional measurements with the same denominator. Interpret information presented to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand line plots that show measurement data, including fractions, and use them to answer questions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can look at data, show it in different ways, and explain what it means.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom, school or community. Determine strategies for collecting or considering data involving addition and subtraction of fractions that can naturally answer questions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions to investigate, decide how to collect data, and use line plots to answer questions, even when fractions are involved.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to investigate and collect or look at data to find answers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts.
description_plain: Your child understands that you can add angle measures together, and that the total angle is the sum of its parts.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles with a protractor and draw angles of a certain size.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint. Understand and apply concepts of angle measurement.
description_plain: Your child knows that angles are made when two lines meet at a point and understands how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
description_plain: Your child understands what angles are and how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in authentic contexts and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply knowledge of the four operations and relative size of measurement units to solve problems in authentic contexts that include familiar fractions or decimals.
description_plain: Your child can solve measurement problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, even with fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units and express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child knows the sizes of different measurement units and can change measurements from one unit to another.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring and changing between different units.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw a line of symmetry for a two dimensional figure.
description_plain: Your child can find and draw a line of symmetry in a shape.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size.
description_plain: Your child can sort shapes by looking for parallel or perpendicular lines, or by checking the sizes of their angles.

subject: Math
description: Explore, investigate, and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can find, draw, and name points, lines, segments, rays, angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines, and spot them in shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
description_plain: Your child can draw and find lines and angles, and sort shapes by their lines and angles.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100. Compare two decimals to hundredths place by reasoning about their size, and record the comparison using the symbols >, =, or <.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions like 0.3 or 0.25 as decimals and compare two decimals up to the hundredths place using >, =, or <.

subject: Math
description: Use and interpret decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can use and understand decimals for fractions with 10 or 100 as the bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate and explain the concept of equivalent fractions with denominators of 10 and 100, using concrete materials and visual models. Add two fractions with denominators of 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain that some fractions with 10 and 100 as denominators are equal, and can add two such fractions using objects or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
description_plain: Your child can understand how fractions and decimals are related and can compare decimal numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number. Represent and solve problems in authentic contexts involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number and solve real-life problems using this skill.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction (a/b) as the sum (a) of fractions of the same denominator (1/b). Solve problems in authentic contexts involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction is made up of smaller equal parts and can add or subtract fractions with the same bottom number (denominator).

subject: Math
description: Build fractions from unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can build and work with fractions by putting together smaller equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and/or different denominators, record the results with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions, even if they have different top or bottom numbers, and explain which is bigger, smaller, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Use visual fraction representations to recognize, generate, and explain relationships between equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures or models to show and explain how different fractions can mean the same amount.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child understands how to tell if two fractions are equal and can put fractions in order from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description: Use representations and strategies to find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by a one-digit number and figure out the answer and any remainder using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Use representations and strategies to multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit number, and a two-digit number by a two-digit number using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a big number (up to four digits) by a one-digit number, and multiply two two-digit numbers together using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract large numbers using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of place value to solve problems with large numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round large numbers to any place value, like the nearest ten, hundred, or thousand.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Use understandings of place value within these forms to compare two multi-digit numbers using >, =, and < symbols.
description_plain: Your child can read and write large numbers in different ways and compare them using the symbols >, =, and <.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a big number, each digit is worth ten times more than the digit to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works for numbers with more than one digit.

subject: Math
description: Analyze a number, visual, or contextual pattern that follows a given rule.
description_plain: Your child can look at a pattern in numbers or pictures and figure out the rule it follows.

subject: Math
description: Generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can create and study patterns in numbers or shapes.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1-100. Determine whether a given whole number in the range of 1-100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number, and whether it is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all the pairs of numbers that multiply to make a number between 1 and 100, and can tell if a number is a multiple of another number or if it is a prime or composite number.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child is getting to know how factors and multiples work.

subject: Math
description: Solve multistep problems in authentic contexts using whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, including problems where they need to figure out what to do with the remainder.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve problems in authentic contexts involving multiplicative comparison, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that involve comparing amounts by multiplying or dividing, and can tell the difference between multiplying and just adding.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as comparing quantities. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as equations.
description_plain: Your child can understand and write multiplication equations that compare two amounts.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve different kinds of problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in their work and use those patterns to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use helpful structures or patterns in math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate when doing math.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to solve real-life problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and understand or question someone else's reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both words and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they solve it.

subject: Math
description: Analyze graphical representations and describe the distribution of the numerical data through line plots or categorical data through bar graphs. Interpret information presented to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at graphs like line plots or bar graphs, describe what the data shows, and answer questions about it.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, represent, and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can study, show, and explain information using data.

subject: Math
description: Generate questions to investigate situations within the classroom, school or community. Determine strategies for collecting or considering data involving operations with fractions for this grade that can naturally answer questions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions to investigate, decide how to collect data, and use line plots to answer questions, including those involving fractions.

subject: Math
description: Pose investigative questions and collect/consider data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to investigate and gather or think about data.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume of rectangular prisms to the operations of multiplication and addition. Solve problems in authentic contexts involving volume using a variety of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication and addition to figure out the volume of a box-shaped object and solve real-life problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure the volume of a rectangular prism by counting unit cubes using standard and nonstandard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure the volume of a box by counting how many cubes fit inside, using standard or nonstandard units.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that volume is a measurable attribute of solid figures.
description_plain: Your child understands that solid objects have volume, which means they take up space.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume.
description_plain: Your child understands what volume is and how to measure it for different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Convert between different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system. Use these conversions in solving multi-step problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another (like inches to feet) and use these conversions to solve multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system, like turning inches into feet.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures within a hierarchy based on their geometrical properties, and explain the relationship across and within different categories of these figures.
description_plain: Your child can sort shapes into groups based on their features and explain how different types of shapes are related.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can group two-dimensional shapes by looking at their properties, like the number of sides or angles.

subject: Math
description: Represent authentic contexts and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane. Interpret the meaning of the coordinate values based on the context of a given situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph to show real-life situations and explain what each point means in that situation.

subject: Math
description: Graph and name coordinate points in the first quadrant using the standard (x, y) notation. Understand the coordinate points values represent the distance traveled along the horizontal x-axis and vertical y-axis.
description_plain: Your child can find and name points on a graph using (x, y) numbers, and understands that these numbers show how far to move right and up.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs to solve real-world and math problems by plotting points.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions, including solving problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, and solve real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in authentic contexts involving multiplication of common fractions and mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to represent and calculate multiplication and division of fractions. Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing) by comparing the size of products of two factors.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions, and can explain how multiplying changes the size of a number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understanding and strategies of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fractional products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by another fraction or whole number, and use this to find the area of rectangles with fractional sides.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve problems in authentic contexts involving division of whole numbers that result in answers that are common fractions or mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number, and can solve real-life problems where the answer is a fraction or mixed number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplying and dividing to work with fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in authentic contexts involving addition and subtraction of fractions with unlike denominators, including common fractions larger than one and mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators, including fractions bigger than one and mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators, including common fractions larger than one and mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators, including fractions bigger than one and mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use a variety of representations and strategies to add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths. Relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to the hundredths place using different methods, and can explain how they solved the problem.

subject: Math
description: Use a variety of representations and strategies to find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by numbers up to two digits using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately multiply large whole numbers using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Use whole number exponents to denote powers of 10 and explain the patterns in placement of digits that occur when multiplying and/or dividing whole numbers and decimals by powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can use exponents to show powers of 10 and explain what happens to the digits when multiplying or dividing by 10, 100, or 1,000.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that each place in a number is ten times bigger than the place to its right and one-tenth the place to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify and analyze relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns and graph them on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can make two number patterns using two rules, compare the patterns, and graph the results as pairs of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand patterns and relationships in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write math expressions to show calculations and explain what the expressions mean, even without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions that include parentheses.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve math problems that use parentheses.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child notices patterns in how they solve problems and can explain them.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and structures to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate in math work.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child uses math to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and understand or question the thinking of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both words and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand a problem and keep working on it until it is solved.

subject: Math
description: Interpret quantitative measures of center to describe differences between groups from data collected to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can use averages and other measures to describe how two groups are different based on collected data.

subject: Math
description: Interpret data and answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at data and answer questions about it.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data representations and describe measures of center and variability of quantitative data using appropriate displays.
description_plain: Your child can look at graphs and charts, and describe what the average and range of the data show.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, summarize, and describe data.
description_plain: Your child can look at data, find patterns, and explain what the data shows.

subject: Math
description: Collect and record data with technology to identify and describe the characteristics of numerical data sets using quantitative measures of center and variability.
description_plain: Your child can use technology to collect and record data, and then describe the data using numbers like average and range.

subject: Math
description: Collect and Consider Data.
description_plain: Your child can gather and think about data to learn more about it.

subject: Math
description: Formulate and recognize statistical investigative questions as those that anticipate changes in descriptive data related to the question and account for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and recognize questions about data that expect different answers and can explain why the answers might change.

subject: Math
description: Formulate Statistical Investigative Questions.
description_plain: Your child can create good questions that can be answered by collecting and studying data.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures, including those from authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) for 3D shapes made of rectangles and triangles, and use them to find the surface area of those shapes, even in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the four quadrant coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices and find the length of a side. Apply these techniques to solve problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can plot points to draw shapes on a coordinate grid and measure the sides to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by filling it with unit cubes of appropriate unit fraction edge lengths. Connect and apply to the formulas V = l w h and V = b h to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths to solve problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box with sides that are fractions by filling it with cubes and using volume formulas to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and other polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes. Apply these techniques to solve problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of different shapes by breaking them into rectangles or triangles and use this skill to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world math problems that involve finding area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Graph points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane to solve problems in authentic contexts. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can plot points in all four sections of a coordinate grid to solve problems, and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points that share the same x or y value.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram. Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers and absolute value in authentic applications.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain what it means when one number is greater or less than another on a number line, and use this to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes to represent points on the line and in the coordinate plane with negative number coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can show any rational number as a point on a number line, including negative numbers, and use coordinate grids to show these points.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values. Use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in authentic contexts, explaining the meaning of zero in each situation.
description_plain: Your child understands that positive and negative numbers can show things like direction or value, and can explain what zero means in different situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with positive and negative numbers, including fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Determine greatest common factors and least common multiples using a variety of strategies. Apply the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1–100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor and least common multiple of numbers in different ways, and use the distributive property to rewrite sums.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide positive rational numbers using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add, subtract, multiply, and divide positive fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using accurate, efficient, and flexible strategies and algorithms based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can divide large numbers using different methods that make sense and are accurate.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child can work easily with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Represent, interpret, and compute quotients of fractions to solve problems in authentic contexts involving division of fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve dividing one fraction by another, and explain their answers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to solve problems with fractions dividing by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve problems in authentic contexts that use equivalent ratios, unit rates, percents, and/or measurement units.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and rates to solve real-life problems, including those with equivalent ratios, unit rates, percents, or measurement units.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit rate in authentic contexts and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit rate is in real-life situations and can talk about rates using the correct words.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a ratio in authentic contexts, and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child understands what a ratio is in real-life situations and can describe the relationship between two amounts using ratio language.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent and analyze two quantities to solve problems in authentic contexts. Including those that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity in terms of the other quantity.
description_plain: Your child can use letters (variables) to show and solve problems where two amounts are connected, and write equations to show how one amount depends on the other.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can use math to show and understand how one amount changes when another amount changes.

subject: Math
description: Write inequalities of the form x > c and x < c to represent constraints or conditions to solve problems in authentic contexts. Describe and graph on a number line solutions of inequalities of the form x > c and x < c.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve inequalities (like x > 5 or x < 10) to show limits in real-life problems, and show the solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve equations of the form x + p = q and px = q in problems that arise from authentic contexts for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve simple equations like x + p = q or px = q using positive numbers to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write math expressions to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true. Use substitution to determine which number(s) in a given set make an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands that solving an equation or inequality means finding which numbers make it true, and can check if a number works by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can think about and solve equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions and to determine when two expressions are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to rewrite expressions in different ways and tell when two expressions mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers. Apply knowledge of common mathematical terms to move between the verbal and mathematical forms of an expression including expressions that arise from authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve math expressions where letters stand for numbers, and can switch between word problems and math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number bases and exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve math problems that use exponents, like 2^3, with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with algebraic expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in their math work and use those patterns to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can spot helpful structures or patterns in math problems to find solutions.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate in their math work.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators or rulers, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to solve real-life problems and explain their thinking with numbers, pictures, or diagrams.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math ideas clearly and respectfully discuss and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both words and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will work through math problems step by step and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Interpret measures of center and measures of variability for numerical data from random samples to compare between two populations, and to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can use averages and measures of spread (like range) to compare two groups of data and answer questions about them.

subject: Math
description: Interpret data and answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at data and use it to answer questions they are investigating.

subject: Math
description: Analyze two data distributions visually to compare multiple measures of center and variability.
description_plain: Your child can look at two sets of data and compare their averages and how spread out the numbers are.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, summarize, and describe data.
description_plain: Your child can look at data, describe what it shows, and explain the main points.

subject: Math
description: Collect or consider data from a random sample to compare and draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest.
description_plain: Your child can collect or use data from a random group to make guesses about a larger group when something is unknown.

subject: Math
description: Collect and Consider Data.
description_plain: Your child can gather and think about data to learn more about a group or situation.

subject: Math
description: Formulate summary, comparative investigative questions to gain information about a population and that a sample is valid only if the sample is representative of that population.
description_plain: Your child can create questions to learn about a group and knows that a sample must fairly represent the whole group to be valid.

subject: Math
description: Formulate Statistical Investigative Questions.
description_plain: Your child can come up with good questions to investigate using statistics.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in authentic contexts involving two- and three-dimensional figures. Given formulas, calculate area, volume and surface area.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems using area, volume, and surface area formulas for 2D and 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Apply facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in a multi-step problem to determine an unknown angle in a figure.
description_plain: Your child can use facts about different types of angles to figure out unknown angles in a shape, even in multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between area and circumference of circles. Choose and use the appropriate formula to solve problems with radius, diameter, circumference and area of circles.
description_plain: Your child understands how area and circumference of circles are related and can use the right formula to solve problems about circles.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems in authentic contexts involving angle measure, area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life math problems involving angles, area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Draw triangles from three measures of angles or sides. Understand the possible side lengths and angle measures that determine a unique triangle, more than one triangle, or no triangle.
description_plain: Your child can draw triangles using three given sides or angles and knows when these measurements make one triangle, more than one, or none.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures. Reproduce a scale drawing at a different scale and compute actual lengths and areas from a scale drawing.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with scale drawings, make new scale drawings, and figure out actual sizes from a drawing.

subject: Math
description: Draw, construct, and describe geometrical figures and describe the relationships between them.
description_plain: Your child can draw and describe shapes and explain how they relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Understand that equivalent rational numbers can be written as fractions, decimals and percents.
description_plain: Your child understands that the same rational number can be written as a fraction, decimal, or percent.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division and of fractions to multiply and divide rational numbers. Interpret operations of rational numbers solving problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide positive and negative numbers, including fractions, and use these skills to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition, subtraction and absolute value to add and subtract rational numbers in authentic contexts. Understand subtraction as adding the additive inverse, p – q = p + (–q).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract positive and negative numbers, including fractions, and understands how subtraction is related to adding the opposite.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations with fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about fractions to solve problems with all types of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of two or more things happening by using lists, tables, tree diagrams, or simulations.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model and use it to find probabilities of events. Compare theoretical and experimental probabilities and explain possible sources of discrepancy if any exists.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to find the chance of events happening, compare expected results to actual results, and explain any differences.

subject: Math
description: Use experimental data and theoretical probability to make predictions. Understand the probability predictions may not be exact.
description_plain: Your child can use data and probability to make predictions, and understands that predictions may not always be exact.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 that expresses the likelihood of the event occurring. Represent probabilities as fractions, decimals, and percents.
description_plain: Your child understands that probability is a number between 0 and 1 and can show it as a fraction, decimal, or percent.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.
description_plain: Your child can explore and use probability to understand and predict chance events.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve ratio and percent problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use proportional relationships to solve real-life problems with ratios and percents.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships. Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) within various representations.
description_plain: Your child can spot and show proportional relationships in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and words, and can find the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in authentic contexts involving unit rates associated with ratios of fractions.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems that involve unit rates with ratios of fractions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve mathematical problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use proportional relationships to solve real-life math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities and construct one- and two-step linear inequalities with positive rational numbers to solve authentic problems by reasoning about the quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use variables to write and solve one- and two-step inequalities with positive numbers to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve problems in authentic contexts using expressions and equations with positive and negative rational numbers in any form. Contexts can be limited to those that can be solved with one or two-step linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve real-life problems using equations and expressions with positive and negative numbers, usually in one or two steps.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems in authentic contexts using numerical and algebraic expressions and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world math problems using numbers, expressions, and equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand that rewriting an expression in different forms in a contextual problem can show how quantities are related.
description_plain: Your child understands that writing an expression in different ways can help show how amounts are related in a problem.

subject: Math
description: Identify and write equivalent expressions with rational numbers by applying associative, commutative, and distributive properties.
description_plain: Your child can find and write different expressions that mean the same thing using properties like grouping and order.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create different but equal expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in their math work and use those patterns to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can spot and use helpful structures or patterns in math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child can use math words and numbers carefully and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child will choose the right tools, like calculators or rulers, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to represent and solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking clearly and understand or question the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think about numbers and problems in different ways, both with and without pictures or symbols.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will work through math problems step by step and keep trying, even when they are challenging.

subject: Math
description: Interpret scatter plots for bivariate quantitative data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities to answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at scatter plots (graphs with dots) to find patterns between two things and answer questions about them.

subject: Math
description: Interpret data and answer investigative questions.
description_plain: Your child can look at data and use it to answer questions they are investigating.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns of association between two quantitative or categorical variables and reason about distributions to compare groups.
description_plain: Your child can find and compare patterns between two sets of data, like comparing groups or types of information.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, summarize, and describe data.
description_plain: Your child can look at data, describe what it shows, and explain the main ideas.

subject: Math
description: Collect or consider data using surveys and measurements to capture patterns of association, and critically analyze data collection methods.
description_plain: Your child can gather data using surveys or measurements, notice patterns, and think carefully about how the data was collected.

subject: Math
description: Collect and Consider Data.
description_plain: Your child can collect and think about data to learn new information.

subject: Math
description: Formulate statistical investigative questions to articulate research topics and uncover patterns of association seen in bivariate categorical data.
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions for a research project and look for patterns in data that has two categories.

subject: Math
description: Formulate Statistical Investigative Questions.
description_plain: Your child can create good questions to guide a data investigation.

subject: Math
description: Choose and use the appropriate formula for the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres to solve problems in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can pick and use the right formula to find the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Solve mathematical problems in authentic contexts involving volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems by finding the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem in authentic contexts to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to figure out missing side lengths in right triangles in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between applications of the Pythagorean Theorem and its Converse in authentic contexts.
description_plain: Your child can tell when to use the Pythagorean Theorem or its opposite (Converse) in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.
description_plain: Your child understands and can use the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use informal arguments to establish facts about interior and exterior angles of triangles and angles formed by parallel lines cut with a transversal.
description_plain: Your child can explain facts about angles in triangles and angles made by parallel lines using simple reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is similar to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, translations, and/or dilations.
description_plain: Your child understands that one shape is similar to another if you can turn, flip, slide, or resize it to match.

subject: Math
description: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations and reflections on two-dimensional figures using coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can describe what happens to a shape on a graph when it is moved, turned, flipped, or resized.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is congruent to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, and translations.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are congruent if you can turn, flip, or slide one to match the other.

subject: Math
description: Verify experimentally the properties of rotations, reflections, and translations.
description_plain: Your child can use hands-on activities to show how turning, flipping, or sliding shapes works.

subject: Math
description: Understand congruence and similarity using physical models, transparencies, or geometry software.
description_plain: Your child understands when shapes are the same size and shape (congruent) or the same shape but different sizes (similar) using models or computer tools.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare size and locate on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can use fractions or decimals to estimate where irrational numbers (like √2 or pi) belong on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Know that real numbers that are not rational are called irrational.
description_plain: Your child knows that some numbers, like pi or the square root of 2, cannot be written as simple fractions and are called irrational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Know that there are numbers that are not rational, and approximate them by rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child knows there are numbers that can't be written as fractions and can estimate them using fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities in authentic contexts by analyzing a graph.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and describe how two things are related in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship in authentic contexts between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child can make a math rule (function) to show how two things are connected in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use functions (math rules) to show how two things are related.

subject: Math
description: Understand and identify linear functions, whose graph is a straight line, and identify examples of functions that are not linear.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a function makes a straight line on a graph and give examples of functions that do not.

subject: Math
description: Compare the properties of two functions represented algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or verbally by description.
description_plain: Your child can compare two functions by looking at their equations, graphs, tables, or descriptions.

subject: Math
description: Understand in authentic contexts, that the graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and a corresponding output.
description_plain: Your child understands that a graph shows pairs of numbers, with one number as the input and the other as the output, in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions.
description_plain: Your child can define, solve, and compare different functions.

subject: Math
description: Find, analyze, and interpret solutions to pairs of simultaneous linear equations using graphs or tables.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand the solutions to two equations at the same time by using graphs or tables.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with one variable including equations with rational number coefficients, with the variable on both sides, or whose solutions require using the distributive property and/or combining like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations with one variable, even if the variable is on both sides or the equation has fractions or needs combining like terms.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve linear equations and pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve and understand equations with one variable or two equations at the same time.

subject: Math
description: Write the equation for a line in slope intercept form y = mx + b, where m and b are rational numbers, and explain in context why the slope m is the same between any two distinct points.
description_plain: Your child can write the equation of a line in the form y = mx + b and explain why the slope (m) is the same between any two points on the line.

subject: Math
description: Graph proportional relationships in authentic contexts. Interpret the unit rate as the slope of the graph, and compare two different proportional relationships represented in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can draw graphs to show real-life situations where two things change at the same rate, understand that the slope shows the unit rate, and compare two different situations even if they are shown in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Understand the connections between proportional relationships, lines, and linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how proportional relationships, straight lines, and simple equations are connected.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide numbers written in scientific notation.

subject: Math
description: Estimate very large or very small quantities using scientific notation with a single digit times an integer power of ten.
description_plain: Your child can use scientific notation to estimate very big or very small numbers, like writing them as a single digit times a power of ten.

subject: Math
description: Represent solutions to equations using square root and cube root symbols.
description_plain: Your child can show answers to math problems using square root and cube root symbols.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of integer exponents using powers of 10 to generate equivalent numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about exponents, especially with powers of 10, to write numbers in different but equal ways.

subject: Math
description: Expressions and Equations Work with radicals and integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child works with square roots, cube roots, and exponents to solve math problems.

description_plain: Your child looks for patterns in their work and explains how they use those patterns to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child notices and uses helpful structures or patterns in math problems.

description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to details and uses accurate numbers and labels in math.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses the best tools, like calculators or rulers, to help solve math problems.

description: Model with mathematics.

description: Plan an investigation to determine the relationships among the energy transferred, the type of matter, the mass, and the change in the average kinetic energy of the particles as measured by the temperature of the sample.
description_plain: Your child can plan experiments to see how energy, type of material, mass, and temperature are related.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design, construct, and test a device that either minimizes or maximizes thermal energy transfer.
description_plain: Your child can design and test a device to control how heat moves in or out.

subject: Science
description: Energy
description_plain: Your child learns about energy, how it moves, and how it can be used.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use models to describe why asexual reproduction results in offspring with identical genetic information and sexual reproduction results in offspring with genetic variation.
description_plain: Your child can show that asexual reproduction makes identical offspring, while sexual reproduction creates differences.

subject: Science
description: Heredity: Inheritance and Variation of Traits
description_plain: Your child learns how traits are passed from parents to offspring and why living things are different.

subject: Science
description: Gather and synthesize information that sensory receptors respond to stimuli by sending messages to the brain for immediate behavior or storage as memories.
description_plain: Your child understands that senses send messages to the brain, which can cause quick actions or become memories.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how environmental and genetic factors influence the growth of organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how both the environment and genetics affect how living things grow.

subject: Science
description: Use argument based on empirical evidence and scientific reasoning to support an explanation for how characteristic animal behaviors and specialized plant structures affect the probability of successful reproduction of animals and plants respectively.
description_plain: Your child can use facts and reasoning to explain how animal behaviors and plant parts help them reproduce successfully.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation supported by evidence for how the body is composed of interacting systems consisting of cells, tissues, and organs working together to maintain homeostasis.
description_plain: Your child can explain, with evidence, how the body is made of systems (cells, tissues, organs) that work together to keep us healthy.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways parts of cells contribute to the function.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how a cell works and how its parts help it function.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells; either one cell or many different numbers and types of cells.
description_plain: Your child can investigate and show that all living things are made of cells, either just one or many different types.

subject: Science
description: From Molecules to Organisms: Structures and Processes
description_plain: Your child understands how living things are built and how their parts work together.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for iterative testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create and test models of objects or tools, making changes to improve the design each time.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from different designs to find the best features and combine them for a better solution.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can compare different design ideas to see which ones best solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe what a design needs to do and what limits there are, thinking about science, people, and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Engineering Design
description_plain: Your child understands how to solve problems by designing, testing, and improving objects or processes.

subject: Science
description: Ask clarifying questions based on evidence about the factors that have caused climate change over the past century
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, using evidence, about what has caused climate change in the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design a method for monitoring and minimizing a human impact on the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use science to come up with ways to watch and reduce how people harm the environment.

subject: Science
description: Earth and Human Activity
description_plain: Your child understands how people affect the Earth and how we can study and protect our planet.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe how unequal heating and rotation of the Earth cause patterns of atmospheric and oceanic circulation that determine regional climates.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how the sun's heat and Earth's spin create weather and ocean patterns that affect climate.

subject: Science
description: Collect data to provide evidence for how the motions and complex interactions of air masses results in changes in weather conditions.
description_plain: Your child can collect data to show how moving air masses interact and change the weather.

subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can use a model to explain how water moves through Earth's systems because of the sun's energy and gravity.

subject: Science
description: Earth's Systems

description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how Earth's materials move in cycles and how energy makes these cycles happen.

description_plain: Your child understands how Earth's different systems (like land, water, air, and living things) work together.

description_plain: Your child can explain, using information, why digital signals (like computer data) are more reliable for sending information than older, analog signals.

subject: Science

description_plain: Your child understands how waves work and how they are used to send information.

description: Energy

description: Conflict and Cooperation (H.CC)
